data.title: Banking ROE Competitive Benchmarking Analysis
data.subtitle: A detailed 5-year analysis on ROE and ROTE performance, examining competitiveness among Bank ABC, FAB, and GIB.

data.title: 1. Introduction
bullets: Bank ABC (Arab Banking Corporation) is a Bahrain‑based universal bank with a strong presence across the Middle East, North Africa, and beyond. It offers corporate, retail, and t...

Gulf International Bank (GIB) is a Bahrain‑based pan‑GCC universal bank, serving wholesale and retail clients across the Gulf region and internationally. It is majority‑owned by...
Profitability and Capital Efficiency: ROE measures how effectively a bank generates profit from its equity base, serving as a direct gauge of shareholder value creation. ROTE re...

data.title: 2. Methodology and Data…
bullets: Official financial statements and investor presentations published by each bank (Bank ABC, FAB, GIB), including annual reports and interim results through Q3 2025. These provide...
Regulatory and supervisory disclosures where available, such as Basel III capital ratios and tangible equity breakdowns, to ensure consistency in capital definitions across banks.
Industry reports and reputable financial news sources (e.g., Reuters, Investing.com) to supplement and cross‑verify reported ROE/ROTE figures and contextual performance metrics 6.
Return on Equity (ROE) = (Net Profit Attributable to Shareholders) ÷ (Average Total Equity Attributable to Shareholders) × 100 %.

data.title: 6. FAB ROTE/ROE Analysis
bullets: Diversified revenue mix: Non‑interest income surged—22% in Q1 2025 and 41% in H1 2025—boosting fee, commission, FX, and investment income contributions to total revenue 6.
Scale and client activity: Strong growth in loans, advances, and deposits (e.g., loans up 7% ytd in H1 2025; deposits up 4%) supported higher operating income and net profit 5.
Operational efficiency: Cost‑to‑income ratio improved to 22.3% in Q1 2025 from 24% a year earlier, reflecting disciplined cost control and AI‑driven efficiencies 12.
Capital strength: CET1 ratios remained solid (13.4% in H1 2025; 13.7% in Q3 2025), supporting efficient equity utilization without compromising regulatory buffers 5.

data.title: 7. GIB ROTE/ROE Analysis
bullets: Revenue diversification: GIB’s net interest income grew by 5% in 2024 to USD 522.2 million, while net fee and commission income increased by 19% to USD 121.8 million, reflecting...
Cost and risk management: Provisions declined significantly from USD 112.0 million in 2023 to USD 54.9 million in 2024, indicating improved asset quality and risk provisioning 17.
Equity growth: Shareholders’ equity rose by 5% to USD 2.5 billion in 2024 and by a further 6% to USD 2.6 billion by Q3 2025, which may dilute ROE if net income growth does not…
Q3 2025 performance: Net profit for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, increased by 11% to USD 140.2 million, supported by an 18% rise in non‑interest income and controll...

### 9.1. Regulatory environment and supervisory benchmarks (e.g., Basel III, SREP)
The UAE banking sector operates under a robust regulatory framework aligned with Basel III standards. As of end‑2024, UAE banks maintained an average Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1)...

data.title: 10. Performance Gap Analysis
bullets: Diversified revenue mix: non‑funded income accounted for 43% of group revenue in Q1 2025 7. FAB also achieved strong growth in investment banking and markets, wholesale banking,...
Operational efficiency and scale: FAB’s total assets grew 11% year‑to‑date to AED 1.34 trillion, supporting economies of scale 5.
Technology and AI integration: FAB embedded AI across operations, including Agentic AI platform and Microsoft 365 Copilot, enhancing efficiency and service quality 5.
Strong balance sheet and capital discipline: FAB maintained robust CET1 ratios (~13.4%) and credit ratings, enabling efficient capital use 5.
